name: Millet (food medium)
basis: per 100 g raw whole-grain millet (~8.7 g moisture)

components.name: starch
components.source: USDA FDC 169702 (carb 72.85 - fiber 8.5 - free sugars ~1.7, derived); Molecules 2023 doi:10.3390/molecules28176339 (proso millet starch 58-68%)
components.evidence: Dominant carbon/energy source of the grain; ~85% of total carbohydrate is starch

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Subramanian & Jambunathan 1981 J Food Sci doi:10.1111/j.1365-2621.1981.tb04232.x; FAO T0818E (sucrose ~66% of free sugars 0.58-1.53 g/100g)
components.evidence: Principal free sugar of millet grain (~66% of the soluble-sugar fraction)

components.name: raffinose
components.source: Subramanian & Jambunathan 1981; FAO T0818E (raffinose ~28% of free sugars, unusually high for a cereal)
components.evidence: Raffinose-family oligosaccharide, second most abundant free sugar

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: pearl-millet varietal study (glucose 0.32-0.75 g/100g), FAO T0818E

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Effectively absent in ungerminated grain; appears only on malting/sprouting. Retained from FooDB list as trace/near-zero

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Glutamic acid, most abundant amino acid (prolamin-rich)

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: First limiting amino acid in millet

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid of millet oil

components.name: total saturated fatty acids
components.evidence: Mainly palmitic (16:0) with minor stearic; class total

components.name: phytic acid (myo-inositol hexakisphosphate)
components.source: Bhatt & Srinivasan et al.; review PMC3982016; millets 0.50-0.70% (500-700 mg/100g)
components.evidence: Major grain organic acid and phosphorus/inositol store (antinutrient chelating Fe/Zn); midpoint of 500-700 mg range

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Ravindran 1991 Food Chem doi:10.1016/0308-8146(91)90088-8 (21-29 mg/100g); oxalatecontent.com (13 mg cooked)

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Peshuk et al. 2020 BIO Web Conf doi:10.1051/bioconf/20201700039 (detected by CE, unquantified)
components.evidence: Present but not quantified in raw grain

components.name: fumaric acid
components.source: Peshuk et al. 2020 BIO Web Conf doi:10.1051/bioconf/20201700039 (detected by CE)

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Peshuk et al. 2020 BIO Web Conf doi:10.1051/bioconf/20201700039
components.evidence: Detected (increases on heat treatment); not quantified in raw grain

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: Note: pearl/proso millet low-Ca; finger millet (ragi) is the high-Ca millet (~350 mg)

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: USDA alpha-tocopherol; note beta/gamma/delta tocopherols also present in germ oil

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Trace carotenoid; genuine but low. USDA reports vitamin A ~0 RAE

remove.name: Menatetrenone
remove.reason: Menaquinone-4 (vitamin K2 MK-4) is an animal/bacterial form; implausible in a plant grain, which carries only phylloquinone (K1). FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae/Rutaceae (parsnip, citrus); biologically implausible in millet grain. FooDB predicted/mis-mapped

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Microbial fermentation polymer (Streptomyces product used as preservative), not a native grain metabolite

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: Trace Strecker-degradation flavor aldehyde, not a growth nutrient; negligible mass

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Non-physiological diastereomer / duplicate of L-isoleucine already listed; FooDB artifact

remove.name: d-Tocopherol
remove.reason: Redundant with tocopherol vitamin E already captured (alpha/gamma); delta-tocopherol folds into total vitamin E, keep as uncovered not a separate growth nutrient

remove.name: Co2+ / CO2 / H2O / H+ / O2 / Nitrogen / Ammonium
remove.reason: Generic BiGG exchange/background species, not food-composition nutrients; supplied by the medium framework, not the millet matrix

missing_added: specific free sugars: sucrose, raffinose, D-glucose, D-fructose with amounts
organic acids: phytic acid, oxalic acid (quantified); citric/fumaric/succinic (present, unquantified)
L-phenylalanine, L-valine (amino acids missing from current list)
dietary fiber, total protein, total fat, class fatty-acid totals
keep_uncovered: ferulic acid and bound phenolic acids (dominant millet phenolics, no direct BiGG met)
beta- and delta-tocopherol (germ-oil vitamin E isomers)
raffinose (RFO oligosaccharide, if no BiGG exchange present)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169702 (Millet, raw) - macros, amino acids, fatty acids, minerals, vitamins
Subramanian & Jambunathan 1981, J Food Sci doi:10.1111/j.1365-2621.1981.tb04232.x - pearl millet free sugars
FAO T0818E - Sorghum and millets in human nutrition (sugar profile)
Ravindran 1991, Food Chem doi:10.1016/0308-8146(91)90088-8 - phytate & oxalate in millets
Review PMC3982016 - pearl millet phytate content
Peshuk et al. 2020, BIO Web Conf doi:10.1051/bioconf/20201700039 - organic acids in millet by capillary electrophoresis
notes: Millet is a starch-dominated cereal: ~62 g/100g starch is by far the dominant carbon/energy source, with only ~1.7-2 g/100g free sugars (chiefly sucrose, then raffinose, minor glucose/fructose; maltose effectively absent unless malted). The most consequential omissions filled in were the organic acids - phytic acid (~550 mg/100g, the grain's main organic acid and P/inositol store) and low oxalate (~20 mg/100g) - plus niacin and two missing amino acids. Removed FooDB noise: MK-4 vitamin K2, oxypeucedanin furanocoumarin, epsilon-polylysine, a Strecker flavor aldehyde, and a non-physiological isoleucine diastereomer.
